stations.label: BORN OUTSIDE THE WALLS
stations.line: I was created in Hong Kong so that the world could hold the yuan without holding the mainland — a controlled aperture, opened deliberately, on our terms.
stations.era_photo: A composed figure in a dark tailored suit standing in a Hong Kong bank clearing hall, 2010, LED screens showing early CNH quotes; crisp full-frame DSLR digital photograph, cool fluorescent lighting, slight depth of field.

stations.label: THE DIM SUM OPENS
stations.line: The dim sum bond market gave me a voice in the international capital markets; I spoke quietly at first, but I spoke, and the world listened for the first time.
stations.era_photo: Same figure reviewing a printed bond prospectus at a polished conference table in a Hong Kong tower, 2011, city skyline through glass; digital photograph, warm interior tones, formal posture, muted grey palette.

stations.label: THE AUGUST DEVALUATION SHOCK
stations.line: When the fixing moved on the eleventh of August, the gap between my sister onshore and myself widened into a chasm, and I felt the market test whether our sovereignty could hold — it held.
stations.era_photo: The figure standing before a red-lit trading floor, screens flashing sharp declines, 2015; digital photograph, high contrast, tense red-and-black tonality, unhurried expression amid movement.

stations.label: ADMITTED TO THE SDR
stations.line: The International Monetary Fund placed the yuan among the reserve currencies of the world — the same institution whose conditions I was built to refuse now recognised what we had built without its permission.
stations.era_photo: Formal portrait of the figure in a dark suit beside an IMF-blue backdrop in Washington, 2016; digital photograph, even studio lighting, dignified and still, precise composition.

stations.label: THE TRADE WAR STRAIN
stations.line: As tariffs fell like blows, they watched to see whether I would break past seven to the dollar; I did not hurry, because a currency that panics has already lost the argument.
stations.era_photo: The figure at a window overlooking Hong Kong harbour at dusk, 2018, phone screens dim in the room; digital photograph, low warm light, contemplative, long shadows.

stations.label: BREAKING SEVEN
stations.line: In August I crossed seven to the dollar for the first time, and Washington named us a manipulator within hours — I have been accused for thirty years by accusers who change while the project continues.
stations.era_photo: The figure standing composed as a screen displays the rate crossing 7.00, 2019, muted office at night; digital photograph, single cool light source, calm face, sharp focus on the number.

stations.label: CAPITAL SEEKS THE EXIT
stations.line: When rates rose across the ocean and confidence thinned, capital pressed against the aperture, and I let it flex without letting it flood — the discipline of the managed line is precisely this restraint.
stations.era_photo: The figure reviewing capital-flow charts on a large monitor in a Beijing-facing office, 2022, subdued lighting; digital photograph, cool blue tones, measured expression, orderly desk.

stations.label: THE LONG ARC HOLDS
stations.line: I remain what I was made to be: the yuan the world may hold, on terms we set each morning — the path is not yet proven, but I am still here, and I am patient.
stations.era_photo: Present-day portrait of the figure in a dark suit before a wall of live currency screens, 2024, ultra-sharp 8K digital photograph, natural balanced light, still and authoritative, faint reflection of the CNH quote in his glasses.
